description: Despite the long distances, the excelllent lighting allowed for surprisingly reasonable views. The bird was initially seen flying by, with sun directly to my back. At that time, I noted a bird heftier than a typical HERG (more like a GWGU in build in flight) with a black mantle. I was able to see the "string of pearls" above and below, and note that the flight feathers were dusky (not black or blackish) beneath, except between the "pearls" and the edge of the tip of the wing... a small area. I then immediately started shooting photos. We refound the bird well out on the reservoir near sunset. Then we watched it for ten minutes or more. It was stockier than the nearby HERG, with short primaries and a huge tertial crescent. It was easy to pick out due to its black back. The bill appeared straw colored. Precise size of bill not determined. Presence or absence of head marking hard to tell with certainty.
